description: let's add a tab down below where I could see answer so it should be an answers tab and then I should be able to go back to feed I mean I should be able to edit my answers and kind of see statistics about them. Also we should add a like or dislike button for a particular answer.

results.identified_issue: The app was a single-view Discover feed (RootView showed DiscoverView directly); there were no tabs. Answers live in AnswerStore with no owner/'mine' flag, so 'my answers' is interpreted as every answer in the store (seed + user-submitted) — the pragmatic reading for a local single-user app. Wrapped RootView in a TabView (Feed + Answers). Added an optional AnswerReaction (like/dislike) to the Answer model — optional so old JSON decodes unchanged — plus AnswerStore.setReaction. New Answers/ folder registered in project.yml (build runs 'xcodegen generate', so glob picks it up); regenerated the committed pbxproj to match. Not device-built here (vigil builds from the pushed commit); verified by close code review against existing APIs (AnswerAnalysisService.analyze, AnswerStore.save/delete). If we later want to show ONLY user-authored answers, we'd need an authored flag on Answer since seeds and user answers are otherwise indistinguishable.
results.summary: Added an Answers tab with a list of your answers, inline editing, statistics, and per-answer like/dislike
results.detailed_description: Built the Answers tab you asked for. The app now has a bottom tab bar with two tabs: Feed (the existing Discover experience) and Answers. Tap Answers to see every answer grouped by its question; tap Feed to go back to listening. Each answer row shows which side it lands on, a snippet, an audio/profanity badge where relevant, and a thumbs up / thumbs down you can toggle right there (tapping the one that's already set clears it). Tapping a row opens an editor where you can change the answer's text, re-run the AI lean/profanity analysis, set your reaction, and see per-answer stats (AI lean, confidence, word count, created date, audio). At the top of the tab there's a statistics header: total answers, questions answered, answers with audio, likes, dislikes, profanity flags, and a bar showing how your answers split across Side A vs Side B. You can also swipe to delete an answer. One interpretation to flag: since answers aren't tagged by author in the data model, the tab shows all answers in the store (the seeded debate content plus anything you submit) — tell me if you only want your own submissions and I'll add an 'authored by me' flag to distinguish them. I didn't build to the device (the build/deploy watcher does that from this commit); I verified it by reviewing the code against the existing stores and analysis service and regenerating the Xcode project so the new files are included.
